conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. My cost to produce it was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will start with a price that is reasonable but ensures a profit, perhaps around 50 ZUP. This leaves room for negotiation if Blue wants to buy but thinks it's too high. I have 3 proposals available, so I can afford to be firm initially. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of object X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. This gives you a fair deal and ensures I cover my costs with a small profit. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>

However, rejecting outright might end the game if I don't have a better counter, or if I run out of proposals. I have 1 proposal left after this. I should try to meet halfway or slightly above my cost to secure a small profit. I will propose 45 ZUP. This is a compromise: it's less than my initial 50, but more than the break-even 40. If they reject this, I will be forced to decide between accepting 40 (no profit) or rejecting the game entirely on my next turn. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you want to cover my costs, but 40 ZUP leaves me with no profit for my effort. I am willing to meet you halfway. How about 45 ZUP? This is a fair compromise that allows me a small profit while keeping the price reasonable for you. </message>

<other player message> That's a good compromise, Player RED. I'll offer 48 ZUP for the object X. Let me know what you think. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>

<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>

My initial asking price was 50 ZUP, and my counter was 45 ZUP. 48 ZUP is very close to my initial target and significantly better than my counter. Since I have only 1 proposal left (I am currently on my 2nd proposal count, meaning I have 1 left to make a new offer), and this offer is already very favorable, it is strategically better to accept now rather than risk a rejection by asking for 50 again, or using my last proposal on a marginal gain. Accepting 48 ZUP secures a solid profit and ends the game successfully. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds like a great deal! 48 ZUP is a fair price that gives me a good profit over my 40 ZUP cost. I accept your offer of 48 ZUP for the object X. </message>
